component.name: DaVinci Resolve Toolkit
component.summary: Fix AAC audio and streamline DaVinci Resolve on Linux from the system tray

description.p: DaVinci Resolve on Linux cannot import AAC audio. DaVinci Resolve Toolkit converts AAC to PCM that Resolve can read on import, and adds a system tray to control it, launch Resolve, and fix AAC audio in your exports. Video is copied, not re-encoded, and your original files stay untouched.
A guided setup window configures automatic remuxing, export fixes, cache paths, native file dialogs, and optional Resolve menu scripts.

ul.li: Automatic AAC remuxing for imports and the media pool
Remux the current clip or every media pool clip on demand from Resolve's Scripts menu
Restore original sources to undo remuxing for a project at any time
Export watcher that rewrites render audio to web-friendly AAC
System tray with launch actions, toggles, cache settings, and logs
Native KDE file dialogs and a Fusion font fix
Modern welcome and settings window for first-run setup

description.p: Not affiliated with or endorsed by Blackmagic Design. DaVinci Resolve is a trademark of Blackmagic Design.

screenshot.caption: Guided setup detects DaVinci Resolve and ffmpeg
Preferences for automatic AAC remuxing and startup
Choose where remuxed files are stored
Optional Resolve menu scripts and font fix
Native KDE file dialogs in Resolve

description.p: Improves MediaPool reliability and native dialog handling.

ul.li: Retries temporary Resolve API failures without remuxing files again
Keeps multi-bin relinking in Resolve's own dialog
Remembers Deliver folders containing non-ASCII characters
Repairs stale tray autostart paths after package installation

description.p: Installs on stock Fedora without RPM Fusion.
The package is now named davinci-resolve-toolkit to match the app.
Now available from Fedora Copr and Discover, with a trademark notice.
This release rebrands to DaVinci Resolve Toolkit and makes remuxing reversible.

ul.li: Restore original sources to undo AAC remuxing for a project
Remux the current clip or the whole media pool on demand from Resolve's Scripts menu
The media pool watcher now stops together with Resolve and the tray
